=== adversarial-review ===
---
name: adversarial-review
description: "Adversarial QA reviewer. Challenges test validity after all tests pass. Asks 'can this test pass with a broken service?' for each test case. Classifies findings by severity and writes a structured report."
---


=== agent-debate-review ===
---
name: agent-debate-review
description: Post PR comments with BMAD persona attribution, then spawn an adversarial agent to counter-check claims and post replies. Creates a visible debate on the PR for the author to read. Use when the user wants to surface findings on a PR with built-in self-correction.
user_invocable: true
---

=== agent-os:audit-docs ===
# agent-os:audit-docs - Documentation Guardian Agent

## Purpose

Comprehensive documentation integrity guardian that maintains knowledge graph coherence and enforces documentation standards across the repository.

=== agent-os:index-architecture ===
# Index Architecture

Recursively build and maintain index files specifically for architecture documentation (system diagrams, ADRs, design docs) to enable progressive disclosure.

## Purpose

=== agent-os:index-specs ===
# Index Specs

Recursively build and maintain index files for specs documentation to enable progressive disclosure.

## Purpose

=== archive ===
---
name: archive
description: Archive completed project tickets with intelligent promotion of architecture artifacts (ADRs, contracts) to global documentation. Use when user mentions archiving tickets, closing tickets, cleanup of completed work, or moving finished tickets. Handles the full workflow - scanning for unpromoted ADRs/contracts, merging to global docs, and moving to archive folder.
allowed-tools: Read, Glob, Bash, Edit, Write
---

=== bibliotheque-refresh ===
---
name: bibliotheque-refresh
description: Refresh the Bibliothèque after a new Notion export. Compares file sizes against MANIFEST.yaml to detect changed/new pages and re-distills only those pages. Run when a new Notion export lands in notion-wiki/export/.
---


=== bmad-debrief ===
---
name: bmad-debrief
description: "Post-mortem and debrief facilitator. Reads ticket artifacts and runs a structured narrative debrief — what happened, what was done, what the outcome means, and what's next. Mary's meeting, not a status dump."
---


=== create-tickets ===
---
name: create-tickets
description: Create Jira tickets (epics, stories, spikes) with proper formatting, AC quality gate, local scaffolding, and optional Leo review. Use when the user wants to create multiple related tickets, plan an epic with children, or batch-create stories/spikes. Triggers on "create tickets", "create an epic with stories", "plan the tickets", "set up the Jira tickets", "scaffold tickets for this work".
---


=== dev-reset ===
---
name: dev-reset
description: "Reset Supervisr dev environment data. Supports multiple reset profiles (lead pipeline, compliance, etc). Use when the user says 'reset dev', 'clean dev', 'wipe dev data', 'fresh pipeline', 'dev cleanup', or needs a clean slate before re-ingesting data."
---


=== gab-operationalize ===
---
name: operationalize
description: Extract repeatable procedures and tribal knowledge from conversation, then codify as skills or persistent knowledge at the correct scope level.
---


=== gcloud ===
---
name: gcloud
description: "Use when the user asks about GCP resources: checking logs, querying Datastore, debugging deployments, Cloud Run errors, service issues. Triggers on 'check logs', 'what\\'s failing in dev', 'any errors in dev-core', 'query datastore', 'gcloud'. Even if they don't say 'GCP' explicitly but mention a known project alias (dev-core, uat-data, prod-core, etc.) or service debugging."
---


=== gitlab ===
---
name: gitlab
description: Access and query GitLab repositories. List groups, projects, clone repos, and manage merge requests across your GitLab instance. Supports multiple organizations with secure token storage.
---


=== index-context ===
---
name: index-context
description: "Recursively generate or update INDEX.md files across a folder tree. Each INDEX.md provides enough metadata for progressive disclosure: subfolders get a one-liner + pointer, files get a one-liner description. Use when indexing a project, ticket tree, documentation folder, or any knowledge base."
---


=== jira ===
---
name: jira
description: Access and query Jira issues. List issues assigned to you, get issue details, search with JQL, create new issues, and explore subtasks and epics. Use this skill whenever the user mentions Jira tickets, asks about their backlog, wants to fetch or create tickets, check what's assigned to them, look up a ticket key (like KTP-115 or INS-226), search for issues, add comments, transition ticket status, or asks "what's new for me" or "what should I work on". Also trigger when the user says "fetch ticket", "pull in the ticket", "create a story/bug/task", "check comments on", or references any Jira project key.
---


=== location-scraper ===
---
name: location-scraper
description: "Scrape store/business locations from websites and geocode addresses to lat/long coordinates. Use this skill whenever the user wants to extract location data from any website, geocode a list of addresses, build a CSV of business locations with coordinates, or anything involving store locators, franchise locations, branch finders, or 'find a location near you' pages. Also trigger when the user has a spreadsheet of addresses and wants GPS coordinates added, or asks about bulk geocoding. Even if the user just pastes a URL to a store locator page, this skill applies."
---


=== mb-doc-housekeeping ===
---
name: mb-doc-housekeeping
description: Mother Base Documentation Housekeeping — checks CLAUDE.md coverage, staleness, infrastructure sync, and required sections across DAC repos
---


=== morning-brief ===
# Morning Brief Skill

## Invocation

```

=== peon-ping-config ===
---
name: peon-ping-config
description: Update peon-ping configuration — volume, pack rotation, categories, active pack, and other settings. Use when user wants to change peon-ping settings like volume, enable round-robin, add packs to rotation, toggle sound categories, or adjust any config.
user_invocable: false
---

=== peon-ping-log ===
---
name: peon-ping-log
description: Log exercise reps for the Peon Trainer. Use when user says they did pushups, squats, or wants to log reps. Examples - "/peon-ping-log 25 pushups", "/peon-ping-log 30 squats", "log 50 pushups".
user_invocable: true
---

=== peon-ping-toggle ===
---
name: peon-ping-toggle
description: Toggle peon-ping sound notifications on/off. Use when user wants to mute, unmute, pause, or resume peon sounds during a Claude Code session. Also handles config changes like volume, pack rotation, categories — any peon-ping setting.
user_invocable: true
---

=== peon-ping-use ===
---
name: peon-ping-use
description: Set which voice pack (character voice) plays for the current chat session. Automatically enables agentskill rotation mode if not already set. Use when user wants a specific character voice like GLaDOS, Peon, or Kerrigan for this conversation.
user_invocable: true
license: MIT

=== post-comment ===
---
name: post-comment
description: "Post externally visible content (PR comments, Jira comments, Slack messages, deploy notices) through a safe pipeline: draft on disk, template render, preview, explicit approval, audit log. Supports editing existing posts. Use when user wants to post a comment, reply to a PR, update Jira, or send any external message."
agent: post-comment
---

=== pr-review ===
---
name: pr-review
description: "Review a pull request following the established protocol: clone/fetch branch to clean location, read Jira AC, review source files. Never review from local working tree. Use when reviewing PRs or merge requests."
---


=== pre-flight ===
---
name: pre-flight
description: "Universal infrastructure pre-flight checks. Validates repo cleanliness, Docker health, credential freshness, disk space, network reachability, and agent definition well-formedness. Severity levels can be overridden by crawl profiles."
---


=== pre-ship-check ===
---
name: pre-ship-check
description: "Pre-shipping readiness check across all services in an epic. Reads REPO_MAPPING.yaml and runs git status on each mapped repo. Reports unpushed commits, dirty files, branch state, and tag history. Use before shipping or deploying."
---


=== push-adr ===
# push-adr — Push Architectural Decisions to agent-os

Formalize an architectural decision by creating an ADR and updating all related specifications in a repository's `agent-os/` tree.

## Usage

=== ralph-loop-preflight ===
---
name: ralph-loop-preflight
description: "Validate prerequisites and configure parameters before launching a ralph-loop. Checks that the target agent exists, state file is well-formed, completion promise is reachable, verification gates are defined, specs/scenarios exist, and scope is clean. Outputs a validated invocation command."
---


=== slack-notifications ===
---
name: slack-notifications
description: Read pending Slack notifications, unread DMs, and mentions. Use when user asks about Slack notifications, unread messages, "what did I miss", or "check Slack".
```

```text
1	# app/ Context: Routing & Progressive Disclosure
2	
3	**TL;DR:** Every service is self-contained. Read the service's CLAUDE.md first, then dive into agent-os/ for specs.
4	
5	## Services (Self-Contained)
6	
7	| Service | Purpose | CLAUDE.md | agent-os |
8	|---------|---------|-----------|----------|
9	| **lead-lifecycle-service** | Lead state machine, disposition, call lifecycle | [Read](lead-lifecycle-service/CLAUDE.md) | [Specs](lead-lifecycle-service/agent-os/) |
10	| **retell-service** | Retell AI integration, call webhooks | [agent-os only](retell-service/agent-os/) | [Specs](retell-service/agent-os/) |
11	| **supervisor-query-service** | GraphQL API for compliance queries (EQS) | [Read](supervisor-query-service/CLAUDE.md) | [Specs](supervisor-query-service/agent-os/) |
12	| **supervisor-compliance-engine** | Compliance rule execution, event processing | [agent-os only](supervisor-compliance-engine/agent-os/) | [Specs](supervisor-compliance-engine/agent-os/) |
13	| **compliance-ers** | Event receiver, persistence, projections | [agent-os only](compliance-ers/agent-os/) | [Specs](compliance-ers/agent-os/) |
14	
15	## The Pattern
16	
17	1. **Start here:** Service's `CLAUDE.md` (if it exists)
18	   - Local build (Maven, npm, etc.)
19	   - Environment setup
20	   - Quick dev commands
21	   - Service-specific patterns
22	
23	2. **Go there:** Service's `agent-os/` folder
24	   - Architecture specs (SBEs, APIs, data models)
25	   - Best practices
26	   - Standards and operations
27	   - Never duplicate this in CLAUDE.md
28	
29	3. **Never here:** No Maven registry, env vars, or architecture specs at app/ level
30	
31	## Frontend & Libraries
32	
33	- **origin8-web/** — Monorepo (Angular, NestJS). See [origin8-web/CLAUDE.md](origin8-web/CLAUDE.md)
34	- **libraries/** — Shared Maven libraries. See [libraries/INDEX.md](libraries/INDEX.md) (indexed, no CLAUDE.md needed)
35	- **landingpage/** — Static marketing site
36	
37	## Development Workflow
38	
39	1. Find your service in the table above
40	2. Clone the repo if not already cloned
41	3. **Read the service's CLAUDE.md** (build, env vars, local setup)
42	4. **Read agent-os/ specs** (if you need architecture or design context)
43	5. Code → Test → Commit → Push
44	
45	For deployment, see `~/.claude/context/shipping-workflow.md`.
46
```
</details></tool-use>

 `/Users/gabrielamyot/Developer/supervisr-ai/project-management/CLAUDE.md`

```text
1	# CLAUDE Context: Project Management Master Guide
2	
3	## CRITICAL: File Placement Rules (AI Agents MUST Follow)
4	
5	**NEVER create files in the project-management root directory.** The root contains only:
6	- `CLAUDE.md` (this file)
7	- `GEMINI.md` (Gemini config)
8	- `.repo-index.yaml` (service graph index — maps all services to DAC/IAC repos, generated by `/validate-repo-links reindex`)
9	- Subdirectories: `tickets/`, `documentation/`, `general/`, `archive/`, `tools/`
10	
11	**Before creating ANY file, determine its ticket context:**
12	1. Identify which ticket (e.g., SPV-3, MVP-001) the work belongs to
13	2. Place the file inside `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/`
14	3. If the file is a long-lived reference (ADR, contract), place it in `documentation/`
15	4. If the file is a strategic/business artifact, place it in `general/`
16	5. If no ticket context exists yet, **create the ticket folder first**, then write the file inside it
17	
18	**Mapping of document types to locations:**
19	| Document Type | Location |
20	|---|---|
21	| Architecture specs, design docs | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/architecture/` (draft) → `documentation/architecture/` (final) |
22	| Implementation plans, checklists | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/architecture/` |
23	| Implementation summaries, release notes | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/implementation/` |
24	| Review reports, adversarial findings | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/reviews/` |
25	| Spike summaries, completion reports | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/` or `tickets/{EPIC}/{SPIKE-ID}/reports/` |
26	| Status tracking, progress logs | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/status/` |
27	| Jira ticket specs, dependency logs | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/` (root level OK for specs/mapping files) |
28	| Jira local data (AC, metadata, comments) | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/jira/` (managed by `/jira` skill or `ticket-manager` agent) |
29	| Meeting notes | `general/meetings/` or `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/meeting_notes/` |
30	| ADRs | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/architecture/adr/` (draft) → `documentation/architecture/adr/` (promoted) |
31	| Contracts / data models | `documentation/architecture/contracts/` |
32	
33	**Reports Convention:**
34	All AI-generated reports, summaries, and tracking documents MUST go under `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/` organized by type:
35	- `reports/architecture/` - Design docs, implementation plans, architecture specs
36	- `reports/implementation/` - Implementation summaries, release notes, deployment configs
37	- `reports/reviews/` - Code reviews, adversarial reviews, findings
38	- `reports/status/` - Progress logs, status snapshots, task summaries
39	- `reports/ship/` - Shipping pipeline reports (validation, review, release, deploy) — used by `supervisr-ship` agent
40	
41	**If you are unsure where a file goes, ask the user. Do NOT default to the root.**
42	
43	---
44	
45	## Directory Structure & Philosophy
46	
47	```
48	project-management/
49	├── CLAUDE.md             # THIS FILE - rules and structure (stays at root)
50	├── GEMINI.md             # Gemini config (stays at root)
51	├── tickets/              # Execution (time-bound work) - one folder per ticket/epic
52	│   ├── SPV-3/           # Example: Epic with sub-tickets (SPV-4..SPV-8, SPV-21..SPV-23)
53	│   │   ├── README.md
54	│   │   ├── REPO_MAPPING.yaml
55	│   │   ├── STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml  # Current state front loader for agents
56	│   │   ├── reports/     # All generated reports/summaries
57	│   │   │   ├── architecture/    # Design docs, plans, specs
58	│   │   │   ├── implementation/  # Implementation summaries, release notes
59	│   │   │   ├── reviews/         # Review findings, adversarial reports
60	│   │   │   ├── status/          # Progress logs, snapshots
61	│   │   │   └── ship/            # Shipping pipeline reports
62	│   │   ├── SPV-4/
63	│   │   │   ├── README.md
64	│   │   │   ├── jira/            # Local Jira data (ac.yaml, ticket.yaml, etc.)
65	│   │   │   └── reports/
66	│   │   └── SPV-8/
67	│   │       ├── README.md
68	│   │       ├── jira/            # Source of truth for AC-weighted completion
69	│   │       └── reports/
70	│   ├── MVP-epic/
71	│   │   ├── MVP-000/     # Planning: "What to build"
72	│   │   └── MVP-001/     # Implementation: "How to build"
73	│   └── ...
74	├── documentation/        # Knowledge Base (long-lived truth)
75	│   ├── architecture/
76	│   │   ├── adr/         # Architecture Decision Records
77	│   │   └── contracts/   # System interfaces & data models
78	│   ├── process/         # Extracted process guides (satellite docs)
79	│   └── tech_stack.md
80	├── general/              # Company Strategy (broad context, NOT ticket-specific)
81	│   └── meetings/         # Strategic meeting notes & DECISIONS_LOG.md
82	├── archive/              # Completed/deprecated work (date-stamped folders)
83	└── tools/                # Shared tooling configs
84	```
85	
86	**Nothing else belongs at the root level. No loose .md files, no summaries, no reports.**
87	
88	---
89	
90	## Core Workflow (Summary)
91	
92	| Phase | Input | Output | Location |
93	|---|---|---|---|
94	| Capture | Meeting | Notes | `general/meetings/` or `tickets/{ID}/meeting_notes/` |
95	| Synthesize | Notes | Decisions | `DECISIONS_LOG.md` (appropriate scope) |
96	| Formalize | Decision | ADR | `tickets/{ID}/architecture/adr/` → `documentation/` |
97	| Define | Decision | Contract | `documentation/architecture/contracts/` |
98	
99	**Need details?** → [Core Workflow Guide](documentation/process/core-workflow-guide.md) covers:
100	- How to write and update DECISIONS_LOG.md entries
101	- ADR template (MADR format) and naming convention
102	- Contract update process and alignment checks
103	- Multi-service deployment ordering (ERS → EQS → Lead Lifecycle → Retell)
104	
105	---
106	
107	## Promotion Rules (Ticket → Global)
108	
109	**MUST DO before marking a ticket complete:**
110	1. **Move/Merge** ticket-local architecture artifacts:
111	   - `tickets/{ID}/architecture/adr/*.md` → `documentation/architecture/adr/`
112	   - `tickets/{ID}/architecture/contracts/*` → `documentation/architecture/contracts/`
113	2. **Update** global `DECISIONS_LOG.md` to reflect promoted changes
114	3. **Verify** documentation reflects actual implemented state
115	
116	**Why?** Ticket folders are time-bound context. Global documentation is permanent truth.
117	
118	---
119	
120	## When to Create ADRs / Update Contracts
121	
122	| Action | YES | NO |
123	|---|---|---|
124	| **Create ADR** | Chose between architectural patterns, selected core technology, changed fundamental data model, security/compliance decision | Implementation details, tactical code choices, bug fixes, minor refactoring |
125	| **Update Contracts** | GraphQL schema changes, API endpoints added/modified/removed, data entity fields change, frontend-backend interface shifts, compliance engine output format changes | Internal implementation changes that don't affect interfaces |
126	
127	**Contract files:** `documentation/architecture/contracts/` (DATA_MODEL_AND_MAPPING.md, complianceEngine/*.json, frontEnd/*.json)
128	
129	---
130	
131	## Context Flow Rules
132	
133	1. **Downward Flow:** Context flows from general → MVP-000 → MVP-001
134	2. **Constraints:** Implementation (MVP-001) respects scope from Planning (MVP-000)
135	3. **Upward Promotion:** Completed work promotes to documentation/
136	4. **Single Source of Truth:** `documentation/` is authoritative, tickets are drafts
137	
138	---
139	
140	## Ticket Initialization
141	
142	New epics require: `README.md`, `REPO_MAPPING.yaml`, `STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml`, and `reports/` directory structure.
143	Templates in `/tools/templates/`.
144	
145	**Need details?** → [Ticket Initialization Guide](documentation/process/ticket-initialization-guide.md) covers:
146	- Full directory scaffold with all subdirectories
147	- REPO_MAPPING.yaml: when to create, when to update, who uses it
148	- STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml: living vs. historical snapshots, content, archival workflow
149	
150	---
151	
152	## Agent Report Rules (Summary)
153	
154	1. **Read front loaders FIRST:** `README.md` → `REPO_MAPPING.yaml` → `STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml`
155	2. All reports go under `tickets/{ID}/reports/{category}/`
156	3. Categories: `architecture/`, `implementation/`, `reviews/`, `status/`, `ship/`
157	4. Naming pattern: `[prefix-][context-][type]-[date].md` (kebab-case)
158	5. Ticket root-level files limited to: `README.md`, `REPO_MAPPING.yaml`, `STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml`, `DECISIONS_LOG.md`
159	6. **Every document write requires an INDEX.md update.** After creating or modifying a report, update the INDEX.md in the same directory with an entry describing the file (purpose, date, relevance). If no INDEX.md exists, create one. This is mandatory. See the global CLAUDE.md "Context Engineering" section for the reasoning.
160	
161	**Need details?** → [Agent Report Output Standards](documentation/process/agent-report-output-standards.md) covers:
162	- File naming convention with component table and concrete examples
163	- Jira local folder structure (`jira/ac.yaml`, `ticket.yaml`, etc.)
164	- `/status-index` skill usage and AC-weighted completion algorithm
165	- Ticket root-level exceptions (full list)
166	
167	---
168	
169	## On-Demand Context (Project Level)
170	
171	Load these when the context calls for it:
172	
173	| Trigger | File |
174	|---------|------|
175	| Mentioning team members, roles, or domain terms (DAC, SBE, ERS, etc.) | `documentation/org-team.md` |
176	| Service graph, repo paths, DAC/IAC mappings | `.repo-index.yaml` |
177	| System architecture overview | `documentation/architecture/` |
178	| Compliance contracts and data models | `documentation/architecture/contracts/` |
179	| Loading a BMAD persona (by name, role, or task type) | `documentation/process/bmad-persona-guide.md` |
180	| Choosing the right skill/agent/plugin for a task | `documentation/process/skills-and-agents-index.md` |
181	| Understanding domain terms, system capabilities, org structure | `documentation/bibliotheque/INDEX.md` |
182	
183	---
184	
185	## Working with This System
186	
187	### Spike Investigation Workflow
188	When working on a `[SPIKE]` ticket, follow this sequence:
189	
190	1. **Pre-read:** Read all existing ticket artifacts (`jira/`, `reports/architecture/`) before the meeting
191	2. **Brainstorm questions:** Surface pre-answered questions from spike analysis; contribute questions aligned with the team's focus (complement, don't duplicate)
192	3. **Meeting notes:** Capture clarifications in `tickets/{EPIC}/{SPIKE-ID}/meeting_notes/YYYY-MM-DD-{context}.md`
193	4. **Post Jira comment** after each significant session using the `/jira` skill with `add-comment`
194	5. **Decision:** Once investigation is complete, update the ticket with a clear proceed / defer / reject recommendation
195	
196	**Question framing:** Match the format and tone of the team's existing questions. Bullet-point format, short, deliverable live in a chat or meeting.
197	
198	---
199	
200	### Starting New Work
201	1. Read this file (`CLAUDE.md`)
202	2. Read ticket front loaders: `README.md` → `REPO_MAPPING.yaml` → `STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml`
203	3. Check `documentation/bibliotheque/INDEX.md` for domain terms and quick answers
204	4. Check `documentation/architecture/` for current system state
205	5. Read relevant meeting_notes/ and DECISIONS_LOG.md as needed
206	
207	### During Work
208	1. **Update STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml** as progress is made
209	2. Update ticket-local DECISIONS_LOG or notes
210	3. Draft ADRs in ticket-local `architecture/adr/`
211	4. Write all reports to `reports/{category}/`
212	
213	### Completing Work
214	1. Promote ADRs to global `documentation/`
215	2. Update contracts if interfaces changed
216	3. Sync global DECISIONS_LOG
217	4. Archive ticket context if needed
218	
219	---
220	
221	## Key Files Hierarchy
222	
223	**Strategic (Business):**
224	- `general/meetings/DECISIONS_LOG.md` - Company direction
225	
226	**Planning (Product):**
227	- `tickets/MVP-000/` - Product requirements
228	
229	**Technical (Engineering):**
230	- `tickets/MVP-001/` - Active implementation
231	- `tickets/MVP-001/MVP-001-IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` - Living execution guide
232	
233	**Permanent (Truth):**
234	- `documentation/architecture/adr/` - Why we built it this way
235	- `documentation/architecture/contracts/` - How systems interface
236	- `documentation/tech_stack.md` - What we use
237	
238	---
239	
240	## Tool & Persona Hierarchy
241	
242	When selecting tools, agents, or personas for a task, prefer in this order:
243	1. **BMAD personas** for process work (analysis, architecture, QA, planning, implementation)
244	2. **agent-os specs** for long-term spec preservation and contract alignment
245	3. **Custom skills** for workflow-specific automation (/ticket-init, /pre-ship-check, etc.)
246	
247	### BMAD Persona Loading (MANDATORY)
248	
249	Never improvise a persona from memory. Always read the full persona file before adopting a role.
250	
251	**Shortlist** (read the file, adopt the persona):
252	- **Winston** (architect): `~/Developer/gabriel-amyot/projects/ai-software-development/dark-software-factory/_bmad/bmm/agents/architect.md`
253	- **Amelia** (dev): `~/Developer/gabriel-amyot/projects/ai-software-development/dark-software-factory/_bmad/bmm/agents/dev.md`
254	- **John** (pm): `~/Developer/gabriel-amyot/projects/ai-software-development/dark-software-factory/_bmad/bmm/agents/pm.md`
255	- **Quinn** (qa): `~/Developer/gabriel-amyot/projects/ai-software-development/dark-software-factory/_bmad/bmm/agents/qa.md`
256	
257	**Not in the shortlist?** Read [BMAD Persona Guide](documentation/process/bmad-persona-guide.md) for the full roster, task-to-persona mapping, unknown persona handling, and multi-persona sessions (`/bmad-party`, `/bmad-party-autopilot`).
258	
259	---
260	
261	## Spec-Driven Development & agent-os Maintenance (Summary)
262	
263	- **Bug fix** → just code, no ceremony
264	- **Tactical change** → `/bmad-bmm-quick-spec` + `/bmad-bmm-quick-dev`
265	- **Architectural change** → full BMAD pipeline (detect phase from ticket artifacts)
266	- **After code changes architecture** → run `/push-adr` to update `agent-os/`
267	- **Ticket closing** → promote ADRs to `documentation/architecture/adr/`
268	
269	**Need details?** → [Spec-Driven Development Workflow](documentation/process/spec-driven-development-workflow.md) covers:
270	- BMAD phase detection: which signals map to which BMAD skills
271	- agent-os update rules: when/what/how to update specs after code changes
272	- `/push-adr` usage and cross-reference requirements
273	- Promotion workflow from BMAD output → agent-os → global documentation
274	
275	---
276	
277	## Self-Correcting Memory
278	
279	When Claude receives a correction or detects a mistake during a session:
280	
281	### Severity Assessment
282	
283	- **Minor** (naming convention, file placement, process step missed): Update the relevant CLAUDE.md with a concise rule. Tell the user: "I'm updating my memory in CLAUDE.md to avoid this in the future."
284	- **Moderate** (repeated pattern, workflow gap, recurring friction): Propose running `/gab-operationalize` to codify as a skill, agent instruction, or README update.
285	- **Major** (architectural anti-pattern, security issue, process failure): Propose creating a dedicated skill or process document in `documentation/process/`.
286	
287	### Rules
288	
289	- Always tell the user what you're updating and why (no silent changes)
290	- Place the update at the right scope level: global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (cross-project), project CLAUDE.md (project-specific), or repo CLAUDE.md (repo-specific)
291	- Keep updates concise — one rule per lesson, not paragraphs
292	- Reference the context that triggered the correction (e.g., "Learned from SPV-3 session: ...")
293	- Never duplicate — check if a similar rule already exists before adding
294	
295	---
296	
297	## Artifact Maintenance Rules
298	
299	### Skills & Agents Index
300	After creating or modifying any skill, agent, or plugin, update `documentation/process/skills-and-agents-index.md`:
301	1. Add/update the entry in the correct workflow stage section
302	2. If it overlaps with existing tools, update the "Redundancy & Overlap Notes" section
303	3. Update decision guides if the new tool creates selection ambiguity
304	
305	### Design Questions Documents
306	When a design document in `documentation/process/improvements/` has open questions that get resolved:
307	1. Add answers inline under each question with `**Answer:**` prefix
308	2. Update the status line at the top (e.g., "Pending" → "All answered. Agent implemented at...")
309	
310	### Reference Document Conventions
311	When creating index/catalog documents (like the skills index):
312	- Always include a "Redundancy & Overlap Notes" section to surface tool overlap proactively
313	- Include decision guides for clusters of similar tools (e.g., "PR Review: use X when..., use Y when...")
314	
315	---
316	
317	## DRY Principle
318	
319	This file defines core rules and decision criteria inline. Detailed procedures are extracted to satellite files in `documentation/process/`:
320	- [Core Workflow Guide](documentation/process/core-workflow-guide.md) — capture, synthesize, formalize, contracts, deployment ordering
321	- [Ticket Initialization Guide](documentation/process/ticket-initialization-guide.md) — epic setup, REPO_MAPPING, STATUS_SNAPSHOT operationalization
322	- [Agent Report Output Standards](documentation/process/agent-report-output-standards.md) — report generation, naming, Jira folder, /status-index
323	- [Spec-Driven Development Workflow](documentation/process/spec-driven-development-workflow.md) — BMAD phases, agent-os maintenance, promotion
324	
325	Subdirectory CLAUDE.md files only add context-specific nuances, not repeat these rules.
326
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-03 23:23:35Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/gabrielamyot/Developer/grp-beklever-com/.claude/CLAUDE.md`

```text
1	# Work Assistant - Project Configuration
2	
3	This project is configured with Work Assistant for work tracking and meeting notes.
4	
5	## Organization Context
6	
7	- **Organization**: klever
8	- **Vault**: /Users/gabrielamyot/Documents/Obsidian Vault/Work Assistant/klever
9	- **Git Root**: /Users/gabrielamyot/Developer/grp-beklever-com
10	
11	## Work Tracking
12	
13	When working in this project, Work Assistant automatically:
14	- Detects org context from `.work-assistant-config/`
15	- Saves work items to: `~/.work-assistant/session-summary-klever-YYYY-MM-DD.txt`
16	- Scans git repos under: `/Users/gabrielamyot/Developer/grp-beklever-com`
17	- Flushes to vault: `/Users/gabrielamyot/Documents/Obsidian Vault/Work Assistant/klever`
18	
19	## Available Commands
20	
21	```bash
22	save-work-item.sh "description"           # Save work item
23	scan-daily-work.sh                        # Scan all repos
24	process-meeting.sh recording.mov          # Process meeting recording
25	generate-weekly-summary.sh                # Generate summary
26	work-assistant.sh                         # Run everything
27	```
28	
29	All commands auto-detect this project's org context.

 `/Users/gabrielamyot/Developer/origin8/app/CLAUDE.md`

```text
1	# Origin8 Engineering Handbook: CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This document provides a comprehensive overview of the Origin8 engineering ecosystem, architecture, and best practices. It is intended to be a living document, maintained by the engineering team, to facilitate onboarding, alignment, and efficient development.
4	
5	## 1. Project Overview
6	
7	The Origin8 platform is a complex, event-driven system composed of microservices and data processing pipelines. The primary business domain appears to be related to the insurance industry, involving concepts such as "carriers," "leads," "advocates," and "transcripts." The system handles data ingestion from various sources, processing, and analysis, with a significant emphasis on data science, machine learning, and business intelligence, as evidenced by the extensive use of Jupyter notebooks and Python scripts.
8	
9	## 2. Architecture
10	
11	The platform is built on a microservices architecture, primarily hosted on Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
12	
13	- **Communication Style**: Services communicate both synchronously (likely via REST or GraphQL) and asynchronously using Google Cloud Pub/Sub.
14	- **Deployment**: Services are deployed as either standalone Spring Boot applications or as serverless Google Cloud Functions.
15	- **Data Storage**: Google Cloud Datastore is the primary database, and Google Cloud Storage is used for file and object storage.
16	- **CI/CD**: The project utilizes GitLab for source control and CI/CD pipelines for automated builds and deployments.
17	- **API Layer**: GraphQL is used for querying data, as seen in the `event-query-service`.
18	
19	## 3. Tech Stack
20	
21	The technology stack is diverse, with a clear separation between the microservices and the data science environments.
22	
23	### Microservices & Libraries
24	- **Programming Language**: Java (versions 11 & 17)
25	- **Frameworks**:
26	    - Spring Boot
27	    - Spring Cloud (including Spring Cloud GCP)
28	    - Spring Integration
29	    - Spring Security
30	- **Build Tool**: Apache Maven
31	- **API**: GraphQL with DGS (Netflix)
32	- **Common Libraries**: Lombok, Logback, JJWT
33	
34	### Data Science & Notebooks
35	- **Programming Language**: Python
36	- **Core Libraries**: Pandas, NumPy
37	- **Environment**: Jupyter Notebooks
38	- **Key Use Cases**: Data analysis, machine learning model training, ad-hoc scripting, and running various data-processing services.
39	
40	### Infrastructure & Platform
41	- **Cloud Provider**: Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
42	- **Key GCP Services**:
43	    - Google Cloud Functions
44	    - Google Cloud Pub/Sub
45	    - Google Cloud Datastore
46	    - Google Cloud Storage
47	    - Google Cloud Artifact Registry (for Maven repositories)
48	- **CI/CD**: GitLab
49	
50	## 4. Coding Conventions
51	
52	- **Dependency Management**: Maven is used for all Java projects. Dependencies are managed centrally using the `<dependencyManagement>` section in `pom.xml` files, often importing Bill of Materials (BOMs) from Spring and Google Cloud.
53	- **Code Style**: The use of Lombok in Java projects is standard to reduce boilerplate code.
54	- **Release Management**: The `maven-release-plugin` is configured for a standardized release process for Java libraries, including versioning, tagging, and deployment to the artifact registry.
55	
56	## 5. Setup Instructions
57	
58	### Java Services
59	1.  **Prerequisites**: Java (11 or 17, depending on the service) and Maven must be installed.
60	2.  **Building**: Navigate to the service directory and run `mvn clean install` or `mvn clean test`.
61	3.  **Running Locally**: Some services may require the Google Cloud Pub/Sub emulator. Start it with `gcloud beta emulators pubsub start`.
62	4.  **Configuration**: Application configuration is managed in `application.yml` files.
63	
64	### Python Services
65	1.  **Prerequisites**: Python and `pip` must be installed.
66	2.  **Dependencies**: Navigate to the service directory and install dependencies using `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
67	
68	## 6. Key Patterns
69	
70	- **Event-Driven Architecture**: The use of Pub/Sub for decoupling services is a core architectural pattern.
71	- **Serverless Computing**: Google Cloud Functions are used for event-triggered processing and other tasks.
72	- **Data-Intensive Applications**: The large `notebooks` directory indicates a strong focus on data analysis, machine learning, and data-driven decision-making.
73	- **Centralized Artifact Management**: Google Cloud Artifact Registry is used as a private Maven repository for sharing internal libraries.
74	
75	## 7. Developer Best Practices
76	
77	- **Release Process**: For Java libraries, follow the `maven-release-plugin` process (`mvn release:prepare`, `mvn release:perform`).
78	- **Versioning**: SNAPSHOT versions are used for development, and release versions are created for stable builds.
79	- **Documentation**: Each service or library should have a `README.md` file explaining its purpose, how to build and run it, and any specific deployment instructions.
80	- **Environment Separation**: The project maintains separate environments for development, UAT, and production, with corresponding GCP projects and resources.
81	
82	## 7.1 Maven Artifact Registry Configuration (Multi-Region Infrastructure)
83	
84	As of January 2025, all Maven artifacts are hosted in GCP project `prj-cmm-n-build-nqzou69e95` in the `us-east1` region. The legacy Origin8-web project is decommissioned.
85	
86	### Repository Configuration (For Pulling Dependencies)
87	
88	All `pom.xml` files should use the **Virtual Repository** for pulling dependencies:
89	
90	```xml
91	<repositories>
92	    <repository>
93	        <id>o8-internal-virtual</id>
94	        <url>https://us-east1-maven.pkg.dev/prj-cmm-n-build-nqzou69e95/are-usea1-maven-virtual-o8-internal</url>
95	        <releases><enabled>true</enabled></releases>
96	        <snapshots><enabled>true</enabled></snapshots>
97	    </repository>
98	</repositories>
99	
100	<pluginRepositories>
101	    <pluginRepository>
102	        <id>o8-internal-virtual</id>
103	        <url>https://us-east1-maven.pkg.dev/prj-cmm-n-build-nqzou69e95/are-usea1-maven-virtual-o8-internal</url>
104	        <releases><enabled>true</enabled></releases>
105	        <snapshots><enabled>true</enabled></snapshots>
106	    </pluginRepository>
107	</pluginRepositories>
108	```
109	
110	### Distribution Management (For Publishing Artifacts)
111	
112	Use **Standard Repositories** for pushing (NOT the virtual repository):
113	
114	```xml
115	<distributionManagement>
116	    <repository>
117	        <id>o8-internal-release</id>
118	        <url>https://us-east1-maven.pkg.dev/prj-cmm-n-build-nqzou69e95/are-usea1-maven-standard-o8-internal-release</url>
119	    </repository>
120	    <snapshotRepository>
121	        <id>o8-internal-snapshot</id>
122	        <url>https://us-east1-maven.pkg.dev/prj-cmm-n-build-nqzou69e95/are-usea1-maven-standard-o8-internal-snapshot</url>
123	    </snapshotRepository>
124	</distributionManagement>
125	```
126	
127	### Local Maven Settings (~/.m2/settings.xml)
128	
129	Add server credentials for GCP authentication:
130	
131	```xml
132	<servers>
133	    <server>
134	        <id>o8-internal-virtual</id>
135	        <configuration>
136	            <httpConfiguration>
137	                <get><usePreemptive>true</usePreemptive></get>
138	            </httpConfiguration>
139	        </configuration>
140	    </server>
141	    <server>
142	        <id>o8-internal-release</id>
143	        <configuration>
144	            <httpConfiguration>
145	                <get><usePreemptive>true</usePreemptive></get>
146	            </httpConfiguration>
147	        </configuration>
148	    </server>
149	    <server>
150	        <id>o8-internal-snapshot</id>
151	        <configuration>
152	            <httpConfiguration>
153	                <get><usePreemptive>true</usePreemptive></get>
154	            </httpConfiguration>
155	        </configuration>
156	    </server>
157	</servers>
158	```
159	
160	### Required Build Extension
161	
162	Ensure this extension is in your `pom.xml` for GCP Artifact Registry authentication:
163	
164	```xml
165	<build>
166	    <extensions>
167	        <extension>
168	            <groupId>com.google.cloud.artifactregistry</groupId>
169	            <artifactId>artifactregistry-maven-wagon</artifactId>
170	            <version>2.2.0</version>
171	        </extension>
172	    </extensions>
173	</build>
174	```
175	
176	### Verification
177	
178	Test your configuration:
179	```bash
180	mvn clean deploy -DskipTests
181	```
182	
183	## 8. Directory Structure
184	
185	The project is organized into three main top-level directories:
186	
187	-   `libraries/`: Contains shared Java libraries that are used by multiple microservices. These are packaged as JARs and deployed to the internal artifact registry.
188	-   `micro-services/`: Contains the individual Java-based microservices. Each service is a standalone application, typically built with Spring Boot.
189	-   `notebooks/`: A large collection of Python scripts and Jupyter notebooks. This directory is further subdivided:
190	    -   `services/`: Contains more production-ready Python applications, often designed to be deployed as Google Cloud Functions.
191	    -   Individual folders (e.g., `geoff/`, `tamas/`): Likely contain exploratory analysis, ad-hoc scripts, and research by individual data scientists.
192
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# CLAUDE Context: Klever Project Management Master Guide
2	
3	## CRITICAL: File Placement Rules (AI Agents MUST Follow)
4	
5	**NEVER create files in the project-management root directory.** The root contains only:
6	- `CLAUDE.md` (this file)
7	- `GEMINI.md` (Gemini config)
8	- Subdirectories: `tickets/`, `documentation/`, `general/`, `archive/`
9	
10	**Before creating ANY file, determine its ticket context:**
11	1. Identify which ticket (e.g., KTP-115, INS-223) the work belongs to
12	2. Place the file inside `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/`
13	3. If the file is a long-lived reference (ADR, contract), place it in `documentation/`
14	4. If the file is a strategic/business artifact, place it in `general/`
15	5. If no ticket context exists yet, **create the ticket folder first**, then write the file inside it
16	
17	**Mapping of document types to locations:**
18	| Document Type | Location |
19	|---|---|
20	| Architecture specs, design docs | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/architecture/` (draft) -> `documentation/architecture/` (final) |
21	| Implementation plans, checklists | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/architecture/` |
22	| Implementation summaries, release notes | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/implementation/` |
23	| Review reports, adversarial findings | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/reviews/` |
24	| Spike summaries, completion reports | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/` or `tickets/{EPIC}/{SPIKE-ID}/reports/` |
25	| Status tracking, progress logs | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/status/` |
26	| Jira ticket specs, dependency logs | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/` (root level OK for specs/mapping files) |
27	| Meeting notes | `general/meetings/` or `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/meeting_notes/` |
28	| ADRs | `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/architecture/adr/` (draft) -> `documentation/architecture/adr/` (promoted) |
29	| Contracts / data models | `documentation/architecture/contracts/` |
30	
31	**Reports Convention:**
32	All AI-generated reports, summaries, and tracking documents MUST go under `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/reports/` organized by type:
33	- `reports/architecture/` - Design docs, implementation plans, architecture specs
34	- `reports/implementation/` - Implementation summaries, release notes, deployment configs
35	- `reports/reviews/` - Code reviews, adversarial reviews, findings
36	- `reports/status/` - Progress logs, status snapshots, task summaries
37	
38	**If you are unsure where a file goes, ask the user. Do NOT default to the root.**
39	
40	---
41	
42	## Directory Structure & Philosophy
43	
44	See `documentation/process/project-structure.md` for the full directory tree and philosophy.
45	
46	---
47	
48	## Klever Repository Map
49	
50	| Purpose | Path |
51	|---|---|
52	| App repos (root) | `~/Developer/grp-beklever-com/grp-app/` |
53	| Frontend | `~/Developer/grp-beklever-com/grp-app/grp-frontend/` |
54	| Backend | `~/Developer/grp-beklever-com/grp-app/grp-backend/` |
55	| IAC (GCP project mgmt) | `~/Developer/grp-beklever-com/grp-iac/` |
56	| DAC (GCP resources) | `~/Developer/grp-beklever-com/grp-dac/` |
57	| Terraform docs | `~/Developer/grp-beklever-com/grp-comp/` |
58	| Project workspaces | `~/Developer/grp-beklever-com/grp-app/_project_workspaces/` |
59	| This project management | `~/Developer/grp-beklever-com/project-management/` |
60	
61	**Jira projects:** KTP (Klever Tech Platform), INS (Insurance-specific tickets)
62	
63	---
64	
65	## Proximity Map Epic Context
66	
67	All Proximity Map tickets (data migration AND frontend/UX) are unified under `tickets/KTP-115/`. See `KTP-115/STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml` for current status and `KTP-115/README.md` for canonical specs.
68	
69	---
70	
71	## Core Workflow (Summary)
72	
73	| Phase | Input | Output | Location |
74	|---|---|---|---|
75	| Capture | Meeting | Notes | `general/meetings/` or `tickets/{ID}/meeting_notes/` |
76	| Synthesize | Notes | Decisions | `DECISIONS_LOG.md` (appropriate scope) |
77	| Formalize | Decision | ADR | `tickets/{ID}/architecture/adr/` -> `documentation/` |
78	| Define | Decision | Contract | `documentation/architecture/contracts/` |
79	
80	---
81	
82	## Promotion Rules
83	
84	Before closing any ticket: promote ADRs to `documentation/architecture/adr/`, update global `DECISIONS_LOG.md`, verify docs reflect implemented state. See Phase 4 in `documentation/process/ticket-initialization-guide.md`.
85	
86	---
87	
88	## When to Create ADRs / Update Contracts
89	
90	| Action | YES | NO |
91	|---|---|---|
92	| **Create ADR** | Chose between architectural patterns, selected core technology, changed fundamental data model, security/compliance decision | Implementation details, tactical code choices, bug fixes, minor refactoring |
93	| **Update Contracts** | API endpoints added/modified/removed, data entity fields change, frontend-backend interface shifts | Internal implementation changes that don't affect interfaces |
94	
95	**Contract files:** `documentation/architecture/contracts/`
96	
97	---
98	
99	## Context Flow Rules
100	
101	1. **Downward Flow:** Context flows from general -> epic -> sub-ticket
102	2. **Constraints:** Implementation respects scope from planning
103	3. **Upward Promotion:** Completed work promotes to documentation/
104	4. **Single Source of Truth:** `documentation/` is authoritative, tickets are drafts
105	
106	---
107	
108	## Ticket Initialization
109	
110	New epics require: `README.md`, `STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml`, and `reports/` directory structure.
111	
112	Sub-tickets require: `README.md` and `reports/` directory.
113	
114	---
115	
116	## Agent Report Rules (Summary)
117	
118	1. **Read front loaders FIRST:** `README.md` -> `STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml`
119	2. Naming pattern: `[prefix-][context-][type]-[date].md` (kebab-case)
120	3. **Update `jira/ac.yaml` after each AC is completed** — this is the recovery save point. If an agent session dies, the next agent knows exactly where to resume. (Learned from KTP-108 overnight session 2026-02-24: agent wrote code for 6 ACs but never updated ac.yaml, making recovery harder.)
121	
122	---
123	
124	## Working with This System
125	
126	### Starting New Work
127	1. Read this file (`CLAUDE.md`)
128	2. Read ticket front loaders: `README.md` -> `STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml`
129	3. **Check `documentation/bibliotheque/INDEX.md`** for domain/vendor/product/stack context
130	4. Check `documentation/architecture/` for current system state
131	5. Read relevant meeting_notes/ and DECISIONS_LOG.md as needed
132	
133	### During Work
134	1. **Update STATUS_SNAPSHOT.yaml** as progress is made
135	2. Update ticket-local DECISIONS_LOG or notes
136	3. Draft ADRs in ticket-local `architecture/adr/`
137	4. Write all reports to `reports/{category}/`
138	
139	### Completing Work
140	1. Promote ADRs to global `documentation/`
141	2. Update contracts if interfaces changed
142	3. Sync global DECISIONS_LOG
143	4. Archive ticket context if needed
144	
145	---
146	
147	## Key Files Hierarchy
148	
149	**Strategic (Business):**
150	- `general/meetings/DECISIONS_LOG.md` - Company direction
151	
152	**Technical (Engineering):**
153	- `tickets/{TICKET-ID}/` - Active implementation
154	
155	**Knowledge Library:**
156	- `documentation/bibliotheque/INDEX.md` - Entry point for all Klever knowledge
157	- `documentation/bibliotheque/GLOSSARY.md` - 107 ad-tech + Klever terms (DSP, TTD, CPIV, etc.)
158	
159	**Permanent (Truth):**
160	- `documentation/architecture/adr/` - Why we built it this way
161	- `documentation/architecture/contracts/` - How systems interface
162	
163	---
164	
165	## Self-Correcting Memory
166	
167	When Claude receives a correction or detects a mistake during a session:
168	
169	- **Minor** (naming convention, file placement, process step missed): Update the relevant CLAUDE.md with a concise rule.
170	- **Moderate** (repeated pattern, workflow gap, recurring friction): Propose codifying as a skill or process document.
171	- **Major** (architectural anti-pattern, security issue, process failure): Propose creating a dedicated process document in `documentation/process/`.
172	
173	### Rules
174	
175	- Always tell the user what you're updating and why (no silent changes)
176	- Keep updates concise -- one rule per lesson, not paragraphs
177	- Reference the context that triggered the correction (e.g., "Learned from KTP-115 session: ...")
178	- Never duplicate -- check if a similar rule already exists before adding
179	
180	---
181	
182	## Memory Governance
183	
184	Memory entries (`MEMORY.md`) are working context, not permanent truth. To keep them useful:
185	
186	- **Promote to CLAUDE.md** when a pattern has been confirmed 3+ times or is older than 2 weeks and still actively relevant. Memory entries are drafts; CLAUDE.md rules are permanent.
187	- **Monthly review:** At the start of each month, scan MEMORY.md for stale entries (resolved blockers, completed projects, outdated feedback). Archive or delete them.
188	- **Target size:** ~20 entries max. If the index exceeds this, consolidate related entries or promote stable patterns to CLAUDE.md.
189	- **No duplicates between MEMORY.md and CLAUDE.md.** If it's in CLAUDE.md, remove it from MEMORY.md.
190	
191	---
192	
193	## Bibliothèque — Klever Knowledge Library
194	
195	Distilled Klever domain knowledge (adtech, vendors, products, stack, ops, people, maps).
196	Entry point: `documentation/bibliotheque/INDEX.md` — card catalog with quick-answer lookup.
197	Glossary: `documentation/bibliotheque/GLOSSARY.md` — 107 ad-tech + Klever terms.
198	Run `/bibliotheque-refresh` when a new Notion export arrives.
199	
200	---
201	
202	## Schema Validation Gate
203	
204	Always verify actual BQ schema before wiring adapters. Full procedure in `~/.claude/library/context/schema-validation-gate.md`.
205	
206	---
207	
208	## Architecture Truth Source
209	
210	- **Repo-specific architecture** (ADRs, contracts, specs) lives in each repo's `agent-os/` directory. This is the primary source of truth.
211	- **`documentation/architecture/`** in project-management is for **cross-cutting decisions only** that span multiple repos or are organizational in nature.
212	- When completing ticket work, promote only cross-cutting ADRs to `documentation/`. Repo-specific architecture stays in the repo.
213	
214	---
215	
216	## DRY Principle
217	
218	This file defines core rules and decision criteria inline. Detailed procedures are extracted to satellite files in `documentation/process/`.
219	
220	Subdirectory CLAUDE.md files only add context-specific nuances, not repeat these rules.
221	
222	---
223	
224	## Stakeholder Question Response Pattern
225	
226	See `~/.claude/library/context/stakeholder-response-pattern.md`. Always check existing Jira comments before posting. Post via `/post-comment`.
227	
228	---
229	
230	## Ticket Housekeeping
231	
232	Three procedures: inventory audit, batch archive, sprint status sync. Full SOPs in `documentation/process/ticket-housekeeping.md`. Run at sprint boundaries.
233	
234	---
235	
236
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/gabrielamyot/Developer/grp-beklever-com/grp-app/grp-frontend/app-front-portal/CLAUDE.md`

```text
1	# App Front Portal - AI Development Guide
2	
3	## Application Overview
4	Next.js 14 frontend application providing interactive proximity-based advertising analytics visualization on Mapbox.
5	
6	**Tech Stack:** React 18, TypeScript, Next.js 14, Mapbox GL, TailwindCSS
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Architecture
11	
12	### Layer Structure
13	```
14	Components (React UI)
15	  ↓
16	Hooks (Data management, event handlers)
17	  ↓
18	Store (Zustand state management)
19	  ↓
20	API Services (fetch-helpers)
21	  ↓
22	Route Handler (Next.js proxy)
23	  ↓
24	Backend API (/proximity-report)
25	```
26	
27	### Data Flow for Reports
28	1. **User selects advertiser/date** → Triggers map store action
29	2. **Map store calls fetch helpers** → `getStatePerformanceData()`, `getCountyPerformanceData()`, etc.
30	3. **Route handler proxies request** → `/api/map/reports/[reportType]` → backend `/proximity-report/api/v1/...`
31	4. **Backend returns data** → JSON with nested structure
32	5. **Transform & store** → `transformDataToCountyNestedStructure()` converts flat to nested
33	6. **Calculate layer expressions** → Mapbox styling and markers
34	7. **Render components** → Map with colored boundaries and popups
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Key Patterns
39	
40	### Report Type Pattern
41	Three geographic scopes: COUNTRY (States), COUNTY, ZIP_CODE
42	
43	**File Organization:**
44	```
45	components/map/
46	  ├── store/map-store.tsx (Zustand store - central state)
47	  ├── types/index.tsx (All TypeScript interfaces)
48	  ├── functions/
49	  │   ├── data-processors.tsx (Transform flat → nested)
50	  │   └── calculate-map-layers/
51	  ├── hooks/ (Event handlers, data fetching)
52	  ├── components/metrics-popup/
53	  └── proximity-map.tsx (Main map component)
54	```
55	
56	### CRITICAL: GEOID Normalization
57	
58	**Problem:** Database vs Tileset GEOID format mismatch
59	- Backend returns: `COUNTY_GEOID: "53033"`
60	- Tileset contains: `GEO_ID: "0500000US53033"`
61	- Must normalize before matching and aggregating
62	
63	**Solution:** Extract suffix after "US"
64	```typescript
65	export const normalizeCountyGeoid = (geoid: string): string => {
66	  return geoid.includes("US") ? geoid.split("US")[1] : geoid;
67	};
68	```
69	
70	**Apply in:**
71	1. `functions/data-processors.tsx` - When accumulating county data
72	2. `functions/calculate-map-layers/calculate-county-layers.tsx` - When matching tileset geoId
73	3. `hooks/useBoundaryClickHandlers.tsx` - When extracting GEO_ID from tileset
74	4. `hooks/useAutoSelectOnZoom.tsx` - When querying county features
75	
76	### Data Structure Pattern
77	
78	**Backend Response:** Flat array with `COUNTY_GEOID: "53033"`
79	
80	**Frontend Storage:** Nested by normalized countyGeoid
81	```typescript
82	{
83	  [advertiserId]: {
84	    [date]: {
85	      CLICKS: { "53033": { Video: 1000, DOOH: 500 } },
86	      CONVERSIONS: { "53033": { Online: { Video: 100 } } }
87	    }
88	  }
89	}
90	```
91	
92	**Key:** All county data is keyed by normalized GEOID (e.g., "53033", not "0500000US53033")
93	
94	---
95	
96	## County Implementation Notes
97	
98	### CountyMapDataItem Interface
99	```typescript
100	interface CountyMapDataItem extends StateMapDataItem {
101	  COUNTY: string;
102	  COUNTY_GEOID: string;  // Format: "53033" (normalized)
103	}
104	```
105	Inherits STATE and COUNTRY from parent MapDataItem
106	
107	### Data Accumulation
108	```typescript
109	const accumulateCounty = (acc, metric, item, value) => {
110	  const { ADVERTISER_ID, DATE, COUNTY_GEOID, CHANNEL } = item;
111	  // COUNTY_GEOID already normalized by backend
112	  acc[ADVERTISER_ID][DATE][metric][COUNTY_GEOID][CHANNEL] += value;
113	};
114	```
115	
116	### Layer Calculations
117	When accessing tileset features, normalize GEO_ID before using as lookup key:
118	```typescript
119	const geoId = feature.properties?.GEO_ID ?? "";
120	const normalizedId = normalizeCountyGeoid(geoId);
121	const countyData = data?.[normalizedId];
122	```
123	
124	---
125	
126	## Map Store (Zustand)
127	
128	**State:**
129	```typescript
130	countyData: ProximityCountyData;  // Keyed by normalized geoid
131	countyCenters?: CountyCentersData; // Keyed by normalized geoid
132	activePopupId: string | null;
133	```
134	
135	**Actions:**
136	```typescript
137	fetchAdvertiserData()  // Orchestrates all data fetching
138	set({ countyData }) // Updates county state
139	setActivePopupId()  // Controls popup visibility
140	```
141	
142	---
143	
144	## Reports Architecture
145	
146	### Report Cards Pattern
147	
148	Reports use a **composition pattern** for extensibility:
149	
150	```typescript
151	// ReportList accepts children for composable cards
152	<ReportList reports={reports} onReportClick={handleClick}>
153	  <DownloadRawDataCard />  {/* Composed, not in array */}
154	</ReportList>
155	```
156	
157	**Benefits:**
158	- Cards can be added without modifying reports array
159	- Keeps report definitions separate from UI extensions
160	- Cards excluded from breadcrumb navigation automatically
161	
162	### Modal State Management
163	
164	Use **URL-driven modal state** for deep linking and persistence:
165	
166	```typescript
167	// URL state hook pattern
168	const { isOpen, open, close } = useExportModal();
169	// Opens: ?action=export
170	// Closes: removes query param
171	```
172	
173	**Why:**
174	- Shareable URLs with modal pre-opened
175	- Browser back button closes modal naturally
176	- State persists across refreshes
177	
178	### API Proxy Pattern
179	
180	All backend requests go through Next.js API routes:
181	
182	```typescript
183	// Frontend: /api/campaigns?advertiserId=331
184	// Proxies to: {BACKEND}/proximity-report/api/v1/campaigns?kleverAdvertiserId=331
185	```
186	
187	**Always forward authentication headers:**
188	- `Authorization` (Auth0 token)
189	- `x-goog-iap-jwt-assertion` (GCP IAP)
190	
191	**File naming:** `app/api/{resource}/route.ts` or `app/api/{resource}/[id]/route.ts`
192	
193	### Long-Running Operations
194	
195	Use **job-based polling** for async operations (exports, reports):
196	
197	1. POST to start job → get `jobId`
198	2. Poll status endpoint every 3s (max 60 attempts)
199	3. On `completed` → trigger download
200	4. On `failed` → show error, keep modal open
201	
202	**Pattern:** Separate endpoints for start, status, download
203	
204	---
205	
206	## Component Patterns
207	
208	### Form Validation Strategy
209	
210	**Poka-Yoke (error prevention) over error messages:**
211	- Disable invalid dates in calendar (don't show "invalid date" error)
212	- Use `disabled={(date) => condition}` on Calendar
213	- Validation on submit, not on button disable
214	
215	**Error Handling:**
216	- Show errors inline, near the invalid field
217	- Keep "submit" button always enabled
218	- Validate on click, highlight problems
219	
220	### Loading States
221	
222	**Progressive disclosure:**
223	```typescript
224	if (isLoading) return <Spinner />;
225	if (error) return <ErrorWithRetry />;
226	return <Content />;
227	```
228	
229	**Use semantic components:**
230	- Skeleton for layout placeholders
231	- Spinner + text for active loading
232	- "Try Again" button on fetch errors
233	
234	### Text Truncation
235	
236	For Select components with long values:
237	
238	```typescript
239	<SelectTrigger className="w-full overflow-hidden [&>span]:truncate">
240	  <SelectValue />
241	</SelectTrigger>
242	```
243	
244	**Dropdown items:** Wrap text in `<span className="truncate block max-w-[400px]">`
245	
246	### Smart Positioning for Floating Elements
247	
248	**Problem:** Fixed-position popovers/dropdowns overflow viewport on small screens.
249	
250	**Solution:** Viewport-aware positioning with flip logic.
251	
252	**Pattern:**
253	```typescript
254	const [shouldFlipUp, setShouldFlipUp] = useState(false);
255	
256	useEffect(() => {
257	  if (isOpen && containerRef.current) {
258	    const rect = containerRef.current.getBoundingClientRect();
259	    const availableSpace = window.innerHeight - rect.bottom;
260	    const requiredSpace = 450; // Expected component height
261	    setShouldFlipUp(availableSpace < requiredSpace);
262	  }
263	}, [isOpen]);
264	
265	// Conditional positioning
266	className={`${shouldFlipUp ? 'bottom-full mb-2' : 'top-full mt-2'}`}
267	```
268	
269	**Safety constraints:**
270	- Always apply `max-h-[85vh] overflow-y-auto` to floating containers
271	- Ensures accessibility even when flip logic can't help (very small screens)
272	- Prevents content clipping
273	
274	**Use cases:** Date pickers, dropdowns, tooltips, popovers, modals
275	
276	---
277	
278	## Configuration
279	
280	### Environment Variables
281	
282	**Required for local development (`.env.local`):**
283	```bash
284	# Backend API
285	KLEVER_PROXIMITY_REPORT_URL=http://localhost:8097
286	
287	# Metabase Dashboards
288	METABASE_ALL_ADVERTISERS_DASHBOARD_ID=199
289	METABASE_SINGLE_ADVERTISER_DASHBOARD_ID=200
290	METABASE_GOAL_PACING_DASHBOARD_ID=397
291	METABASE_CONVERSIONS_DASHBOARD_ID=399
292	METABASE_CAMPAIGN_DETAILS_DASHBOARD_ID=400
293	METABASE_REACH_FREQUENCY_DASHBOARD_ID=398
294	
295	# Development mode (mock permissions + full reports)
296	IAP_IDENTITY_SOURCE=LOCAL_MOCK
297	```
298	
299	**Environment modes:**
300	- `IAP_IDENTITY_SOURCE=GOOGLE` → Demo mode (1 report, auto-select)
301	- `IAP_IDENTITY_SOURCE=LOCAL_MOCK` → Dev mode (mock permissions, 4 reports)
302	- Other values → Production (real auth services required)
303	
304	### Development Setup
305	```bash
306	npm install
307	npm run dev        # Local development (port 3000)
308	npm run build      # Production build
309	npm run lint       # Type checking
310	```
311	
312	### Backend Connection
313	- Proximity Report API: `http://localhost:8097`
314	- Start backend: `cd proximity-report && mvn spring-boot:run`
315	
316	---
317	
318	## Troubleshooting
319	
320	### County data not matching boundaries
321	**Cause:** GEOID format mismatch
322	**Fix:** Always normalize tileset GEO_ID with `normalizeCountyGeoid()`
323	
324	### Popup not showing
325	- Verify normalized `countyGeoid` exists in `countyMapData`
326	- Check `activePopupId` matches expected format
327	- Ensure `countyCenters` keyed by normalized geoid
328	
329	### Boundaries not colored
330	- Check `calculateCountyExpressions()` normalizes geoIds
331	- Verify filter expression uses normalized geoid
332	- Confirm Mapbox layer name matches CONFIG
333	
334	---
335	
336	## On-Demand State Layer Loading (INS-205)
337	
338	**Pattern:** State data loaded on-demand based on viewport, NOT during initial load.
339	
340	**Flow:**
341	1. User pans/zooms in COUNTRY scope
342	2. `useMapMoveHandler` detects movement (300ms debounce)
343	3. `getVisibleStateIds()` extracts visible state abbreviations
344	4. `fetchStateMapData()` calls `/api/map/data/state` with visible IDs
345	5. Backend returns pre-aggregated metrics (clicks, impressions, spend, devices, conversions)
346	6. Map renders colored state boundaries
347	
348	**Key Files:**
349	- `hooks/useMapMoveHandler.tsx` - Viewport listener that triggers data fetching
350	- `store/map-store.tsx` - State management (`stateMapData`, `fetchStateMapData`)
351	- `store/fetch-helpers.tsx` - API wrapper (`getStateMapData`)
352	- `app/api/map/data/state/route.ts` - Next.js proxy to backend
353	
354	**Caching:**
355	- Frontend: Based on `JSON.stringify({ normalizedStateIds, date, channels })`
356	- Backend: 1-hour TTL on `mapStateCache`
357	- Prevents duplicate requests for same viewport
358	
359	**IMPORTANT:**
360	- State data is **NOT** loaded in `fetchAdvertiserData()` (initial load or background load)
361	- State data is **purely on-demand** - only fetched when viewport changes
362	- Old endpoints `/api/map/reports/state-performance` and `/state-reach` **removed**
363	- Use `/api/map/data/state` endpoint instead
364	
365	**Error Handling:**
366	- `MapErrorBoundary` component wraps map for graceful error recovery
367	- User-friendly error messages guide users to refresh or pan map
368	
369	---
370	
371	## Architectural Decisions (Applied)
372	
373	### Composition Over Configuration
374	- Use React children prop for extensible components
375	- Don't add UI elements to data arrays (reports, items)
376	- Keep data models separate from UI concerns
377	
378	### URL as State
379	- Modal state via query params (`?action=export`)
380	- Deep linking and shareability
381	- Browser navigation works naturally
382	
383	### Progressive Enhancement
384	- Start with working skeleton (Skeleton component)
385	- Add loading states (Spinner + text)
386	- Provide error recovery (Try Again buttons)
387	- Never block user with disabled states without clear reason
388	
389	### API Design
390	- Separate concerns: start job, check status, get result
391	- Forward all auth headers through proxy
392	- Use existing env vars when possible
393	- Job IDs in URL path, filters in query/body
394	
395	### User Experience
396	- Error prevention > error messages (Poka-Yoke)
397	- Validation on submit, not on disable
398	- Keep user in context (modal stays open on error)
399	- Show progress (loading spinners, polling states)
400	
401	### File Naming
402	- kebab-case for all files
403	- Feature folders: `components/feature/component-name.tsx`
404	- Barrel exports: `index.ts` for public API
405	- API routes match REST conventions
406	
407	---
408	
409	**Last Updated:** 2025-01-30
410
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/gabrielamyot/Developer/grp-beklever-com/grp-app/grp-frontend/app-front-portal-ktp35/CLAUDE.md`

```text
1	# App Front Portal - AI Development Guide
2	
3	## Application Overview
4	Next.js 14 frontend application providing interactive proximity-based advertising analytics visualization on Mapbox.
5	
6	**Tech Stack:** React 18, TypeScript, Next.js 14, Mapbox GL, TailwindCSS
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Architecture
11	
12	### Layer Structure
13	```
14	Components (React UI)
15	  ↓
16	Hooks (Data management, event handlers)
17	  ↓
18	Store (Zustand state management)
19	  ↓
20	API Services (fetch-helpers)
21	  ↓
22	Route Handler (Next.js proxy)
23	  ↓
24	Backend API (/proximity-report)
25	```
26	
27	### Data Flow for Reports
28	1. **User selects advertiser/date** → Triggers map store action
29	2. **Map store calls fetch helpers** → `getStatePerformanceData()`, `getCountyPerformanceData()`, etc.
30	3. **Route handler proxies request** → `/api/map/reports/[reportType]` → backend `/proximity-report/api/v1/...`
31	4. **Backend returns data** → JSON with nested structure
32	5. **Transform & store** → `transformDataToCountyNestedStructure()` converts flat to nested
33	6. **Calculate layer expressions** → Mapbox styling and markers
34	7. **Render components** → Map with colored boundaries and popups
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Key Patterns
39	
40	### Report Type Pattern
41	Three geographic scopes: COUNTRY (States), COUNTY, ZIP_CODE
42	
43	**File Organization:**
44	```
45	components/map/
46	  ├── store/map-store.tsx (Zustand store - central state)
47	  ├── types/index.tsx (All TypeScript interfaces)
48	  ├── functions/
49	  │   ├── data-processors.tsx (Transform flat → nested)
50	  │   └── calculate-map-layers/
51	  ├── hooks/ (Event handlers, data fetching)
52	  ├── components/metrics-popup/
53	  └── proximity-map.tsx (Main map component)
54	```
55	
56	### CRITICAL: GEOID Normalization
57	
58	**Problem:** Database vs Tileset GEOID format mismatch
59	- Backend returns: `COUNTY_GEOID: "53033"`
60	- Tileset contains: `GEO_ID: "0500000US53033"`
61	- Must normalize before matching and aggregating
62	
63	**Solution:** Extract suffix after "US"
64	```typescript
65	export const normalizeCountyGeoid = (geoid: string): string => {
66	  return geoid.includes("US") ? geoid.split("US")[1] : geoid;
67	};
68	```
69	
70	**Apply in:**
71	1. `functions/data-processors.tsx` - When accumulating county data
72	2. `functions/calculate-map-layers/calculate-county-layers.tsx` - When matching tileset geoId
73	3. `hooks/useBoundaryClickHandlers.tsx` - When extracting GEO_ID from tileset
74	4. `hooks/useAutoSelectOnZoom.tsx` - When querying county features
75	
76	### Data Structure Pattern
77	
78	**Backend Response:** Flat array with `COUNTY_GEOID: "53033"`
79	
80	**Frontend Storage:** Nested by normalized countyGeoid
81	```typescript
82	{
83	  [advertiserId]: {
84	    [date]: {
85	      CLICKS: { "53033": { Video: 1000, DOOH: 500 } },
86	      CONVERSIONS: { "53033": { Online: { Video: 100 } } }
87	    }
88	  }
89	}
90	```
91	
92	**Key:** All county data is keyed by normalized GEOID (e.g., "53033", not "0500000US53033")
93	
94	---
95	
96	## County Implementation Notes
97	
98	### CountyMapDataItem Interface
99	```typescript
100	interface CountyMapDataItem extends StateMapDataItem {
101	  COUNTY: string;
102	  COUNTY_GEOID: string;  // Format: "53033" (normalized)
103	}
104	```
105	Inherits STATE and COUNTRY from parent MapDataItem
106	
107	### Data Accumulation
108	```typescript
109	const accumulateCounty = (acc, metric, item, value) => {
110	  const { ADVERTISER_ID, DATE, COUNTY_GEOID, CHANNEL } = item;
111	  // COUNTY_GEOID already normalized by backend
112	  acc[ADVERTISER_ID][DATE][metric][COUNTY_GEOID][CHANNEL] += value;
113	};
114	```
115	
116	### Layer Calculations
117	When accessing tileset features, normalize GEO_ID before using as lookup key:
118	```typescript
119	const geoId = feature.properties?.GEO_ID ?? "";
120	const normalizedId = normalizeCountyGeoid(geoId);
121	const countyData = data?.[normalizedId];
122	```
123	
124	---
125	
126	## Map Store (Zustand)
127	
128	**State:**
129	```typescript
130	countyData: ProximityCountyData;  // Keyed by normalized geoid
131	countyCenters?: CountyCentersData; // Keyed by normalized geoid
132	activePopupId: string | null;
133	```
134	
135	**Actions:**
136	```typescript
137	fetchAdvertiserData()  // Orchestrates all data fetching
138	set({ countyData }) // Updates county state
139	setActivePopupId()  // Controls popup visibility
140	```
141	
142	---
143	
144	## Reports Architecture
145	
146	### Report Cards Pattern
147	
148	Reports use a **composition pattern** for extensibility:
149	
150	```typescript
151	// ReportList accepts children for composable cards
152	<ReportList reports={reports} onReportClick={handleClick}>
153	  <DownloadRawDataCard />  {/* Composed, not in array */}
154	</ReportList>
155	```
156	
157	**Benefits:**
158	- Cards can be added without modifying reports array
159	- Keeps report definitions separate from UI extensions
160	- Cards excluded from breadcrumb navigation automatically
161	
162	### Modal State Management
163	
164	Use **URL-driven modal state** for deep linking and persistence:
165	
166	```typescript
167	// URL state hook pattern
168	const { isOpen, open, close } = useExportModal();
169	// Opens: ?action=export
170	// Closes: removes query param
171	```
172	
173	**Why:**
174	- Shareable URLs with modal pre-opened
175	- Browser back button closes modal naturally
176	- State persists across refreshes
177	
178	### API Proxy Pattern
179	
180	All backend requests go through Next.js API routes:
181	
182	```typescript
183	// Frontend: /api/campaigns?advertiserId=331
184	// Proxies to: {BACKEND}/proximity-report/api/v1/campaigns?kleverAdvertiserId=331
185	```
186	
187	**Always forward authentication headers:**
188	- `Authorization` (Auth0 token)
189	- `x-goog-iap-jwt-assertion` (GCP IAP)
190	
191	**File naming:** `app/api/{resource}/route.ts` or `app/api/{resource}/[id]/route.ts`
192	
193	### Long-Running Operations
194	
195	Use **job-based polling** for async operations (exports, reports):
196	
197	1. POST to start job → get `jobId`
198	2. Poll status endpoint every 3s (max 60 attempts)
199	3. On `completed` → trigger download
200	4. On `failed` → show error, keep modal open
201	
202	**Pattern:** Separate endpoints for start, status, download
203	
204	---
205	
206	## Component Patterns
207	
208	### Form Validation Strategy
209	
210	**Poka-Yoke (error prevention) over error messages:**
211	- Disable invalid dates in calendar (don't show "invalid date" error)
212	- Use `disabled={(date) => condition}` on Calendar
213	- Validation on submit, not on button disable
214	
215	**Error Handling:**
216	- Show errors inline, near the invalid field
217	- Keep "submit" button always enabled
218	- Validate on click, highlight problems
219	
220	### Loading States
221	
222	**Progressive disclosure:**
223	```typescript
224	if (isLoading) return <Spinner />;
225	if (error) return <ErrorWithRetry />;
226	return <Content />;
227	```
228	
229	**Use semantic components:**
230	- Skeleton for layout placeholders
231	- Spinner + text for active loading
232	- "Try Again" button on fetch errors
233	
234	### Text Truncation
235	
236	For Select components with long values:
237	
238	```typescript
239	<SelectTrigger className="w-full overflow-hidden [&>span]:truncate">
240	  <SelectValue />
241	</SelectTrigger>
242	```
243	
244	**Dropdown items:** Wrap text in `<span className="truncate block max-w-[400px]">`
245	
246	### Smart Positioning for Floating Elements
247	
248	**Problem:** Fixed-position popovers/dropdowns overflow viewport on small screens.
249	
250	**Solution:** Viewport-aware positioning with flip logic.
251	
252	**Pattern:**
253	```typescript
254	const [shouldFlipUp, setShouldFlipUp] = useState(false);
255	
256	useEffect(() => {
257	  if (isOpen && containerRef.current) {
258	    const rect = containerRef.current.getBoundingClientRect();
259	    const availableSpace = window.innerHeight - rect.bottom;
260	    const requiredSpace = 450; // Expected component height
261	    setShouldFlipUp(availableSpace < requiredSpace);
262	  }
263	}, [isOpen]);
264	
265	// Conditional positioning
266	className={`${shouldFlipUp ? 'bottom-full mb-2' : 'top-full mt-2'}`}
267	```
268	
269	**Safety constraints:**
270	- Always apply `max-h-[85vh] overflow-y-auto` to floating containers
271	- Ensures accessibility even when flip logic can't help (very small screens)
272	- Prevents content clipping
273	
274	**Use cases:** Date pickers, dropdowns, tooltips, popovers, modals
275	
276	---
277	
278	## Configuration
279	
280	### Environment Variables
281	
282	**Required for local development (`.env.local`):**
283	```bash
284	# Backend API
285	KLEVER_PROXIMITY_REPORT_URL=http://localhost:8097
286	
287	# Metabase Dashboards
288	METABASE_ALL_ADVERTISERS_DASHBOARD_ID=199
289	METABASE_SINGLE_ADVERTISER_DASHBOARD_ID=200
290	METABASE_GOAL_PACING_DASHBOARD_ID=397
291	METABASE_CONVERSIONS_DASHBOARD_ID=399
292	METABASE_CAMPAIGN_DETAILS_DASHBOARD_ID=400
293	METABASE_REACH_FREQUENCY_DASHBOARD_ID=398
294	
295	# Development mode (mock permissions + full reports)
296	IAP_IDENTITY_SOURCE=LOCAL_MOCK
297	```
298	
299	**Environment modes:**
300	- `IAP_IDENTITY_SOURCE=GOOGLE` → Demo mode (1 report, auto-select)
301	- `IAP_IDENTITY_SOURCE=LOCAL_MOCK` → Dev mode (mock permissions, 4 reports)
302	- Other values → Production (real auth services required)
303	
304	### Development Setup
305	```bash
306	npm install
307	npm run dev        # Local development (port 3000)
308	npm run build      # Production build
309	npm run lint       # Type checking
310	```
311	
312	### Backend Connection
313	- Proximity Report API: `http://localhost:8097`
314	- Start backend: `cd proximity-report && mvn spring-boot:run`
315	
316	---
317	
318	## Troubleshooting
319	
320	### County data not matching boundaries
321	**Cause:** GEOID format mismatch
322	**Fix:** Always normalize tileset GEO_ID with `normalizeCountyGeoid()`
323	
324	### Popup not showing
325	- Verify normalized `countyGeoid` exists in `countyMapData`
326	- Check `activePopupId` matches expected format
327	- Ensure `countyCenters` keyed by normalized geoid
328	
329	### Boundaries not colored
330	- Check `calculateCountyExpressions()` normalizes geoIds
331	- Verify filter expression uses normalized geoid
332	- Confirm Mapbox layer name matches CONFIG
333	
334	---
335	
336	## On-Demand State Layer Loading (INS-205)
337	
338	**Pattern:** State data loaded on-demand based on viewport, NOT during initial load.
339	
340	**Flow:**
341	1. User pans/zooms in COUNTRY scope
342	2. `useMapMoveHandler` detects movement (300ms debounce)
343	3. `getVisibleStateIds()` extracts visible state abbreviations
344	4. `fetchStateMapData()` calls `/api/map/data/state` with visible IDs
345	5. Backend returns pre-aggregated metrics (clicks, impressions, spend, devices, conversions)
346	6. Map renders colored state boundaries
347	
348	**Key Files:**
349	- `hooks/useMapMoveHandler.tsx` - Viewport listener that triggers data fetching
350	- `store/map-store.tsx` - State management (`stateMapData`, `fetchStateMapData`)
351	- `store/fetch-helpers.tsx` - API wrapper (`getStateMapData`)
352	- `app/api/map/data/state/route.ts` - Next.js proxy to backend
353	
354	**Caching:**
355	- Frontend: Based on `JSON.stringify({ normalizedStateIds, date, channels })`
356	- Backend: 1-hour TTL on `mapStateCache`
357	- Prevents duplicate requests for same viewport
358	
359	**IMPORTANT:**
360	- State data is **NOT** loaded in `fetchAdvertiserData()` (initial load or background load)
361	- State data is **purely on-demand** - only fetched when viewport changes
362	- Old endpoints `/api/map/reports/state-performance` and `/state-reach` **removed**
363	- Use `/api/map/data/state` endpoint instead
364	
365	**Error Handling:**
366	- `MapErrorBoundary` component wraps map for graceful error recovery
367	- User-friendly error messages guide users to refresh or pan map
368	
369	---
370	
371	## Architectural Decisions (Applied)
372	
373	### Composition Over Configuration
374	- Use React children prop for extensible components
375	- Don't add UI elements to data arrays (reports, items)
376	- Keep data models separate from UI concerns
377	
378	### URL as State
379	- Modal state via query params (`?action=export`)
380	- Deep linking and shareability
381	- Browser navigation works naturally
382	
383	### Progressive Enhancement
384	- Start with working skeleton (Skeleton component)
385	- Add loading states (Spinner + text)
386	- Provide error recovery (Try Again buttons)
387	- Never block user with disabled states without clear reason
388	
389	### API Design
390	- Separate concerns: start job, check status, get result
391	- Forward all auth headers through proxy
392	- Use existing env vars when possible
393	- Job IDs in URL path, filters in query/body
394	
395	### User Experience
396	- Error prevention > error messages (Poka-Yoke)
397	- Validation on submit, not on disable
398	- Keep user in context (modal stays open on error)
399	- Show progress (loading spinners, polling states)
400	
401	### File Naming
402	- kebab-case for all files
403	- Feature folders: `components/feature/component-name.tsx`
404	- Barrel exports: `index.ts` for public API
405	- API routes match REST conventions
406	
407	---
408	
409	**Last Updated:** 2025-01-30
410
```
</details></tool-use>
